theme: job router for a shop of aging machines
title: Job Router for a Shop of Aging Machines
strategies: trivial: round-robin over machines, ignoring job type/weight/size and every machine's state (~0.1 by construction, matches the evaluator's own weak recipe)

greedy: shortest-queue dispatch -- always send the job to whichever machine currently becomes free soonest, ignoring abrasion/polish sensitivity and job type; load-balances queue length but spreads wear evenly instead of concentrating it, so it never builds a genuinely fast machine and never protects one from cheap abrasive filler -- lands far from strong on the trap instances where filler abrasive jobs are cheap/low-weight and finishing jobs are rare/high-weight or arrive in short alternating bursts

strong: portfolio dispatch -- route abrasive jobs toward whichever machine has the lowest abrasion sensitivity (cheapest to sacrifice) and finishing jobs toward whichever machine has the highest polish sensitivity (deliberately builds a fast machine for the valuable work), with a queueing-overflow safety valve that falls back to real-time earliest-completion when the preferred machine's queue has drifted too far ahead; degenerates gracefully to load-balancing when machines are symmetric
invalid: requests a machine id far out of range on the first job -> the whole instance is voided, scores 0.0
